title: Germany Files Charges in ‘Operation Chargeback’ Payment Scam

title: US Stocks Fall as Samsung Leads Selloff in Chips: Markets Wrap
summary: Stocks fell as volatility hit chipmakers anew after blowout earnings from Samsung Electronics Co. left investors wanting even more. Bonds fell as oil rose by the most in more than a week.

title: China Touts Yuan as US Ups Strong Dollar: 3-Minutes MLIV
summary: Anna Edwards, Guy Johnson and Paul Dobson break down today's key themes for analysts and investors on "Bloomberg: The Opening Trade." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Panetta Sees Fragile Outlook With Risks to Inflation and Growth
summary: The European Central Bank is navigating an uncertain economic situation even as the US and Iran move closer to a lasting peace deal, according to Governing Council member Fabio Panetta.

title: Czech June Inflation Slows More Than Expected After Rate Hike
summary: Czech inflation slowed more than expected below target last month, underscoring the central bank’s monetary-policy dilemma after it raised interest rates.

title: Wall Street Warms to India’s Markets as Oil Pressures Fade
summary: After spending more than a year on the sidelines, global investors are showing fresh interest in India as sentiment begins to improve across the country’s markets.

title: Samsung Results Trigger Stock Rotation to Less Loved Sectors
summary: Asian tech stocks slumped, as investors used Samsung Electronics Co.’s results to lock in profits on a stellar year-to-date chip rally, rotating into unloved sectors less prone to earnings shocks.

title: Hedge Fund Veterans Are Accelerating a Major Shift in Development Finance
summary: A group of hedge fund veterans is helping drive a fundamental shift in how to generate financing for developing nations.

title: Italy’s Oldest Dairy Taps New Way to Borrow Against Aging Cheese
summary: Italy’s oldest dairy company has borrowed money against cheese still aging in its warehouse, taking advantage of a change in the law aimed at helping food and wine producers access finance.

title: Gold Slips for Second Day as Hormuz Attacks Fan Inflation Fears
summary: Gold fell for a second day after renewed attacks on shipping in the Strait of Hormuz, a reminder of the risks faced by vessels carrying oil and natural gas via the waterway.

title: Japanese Bonds Stage Brief Rally After Strong 30-Year Debt Sale
summary: Japanese bonds rallied briefly after an auction of 30-year government debt carrying the first 4% coupon drew the highest demand ratio in seven years.

title: Google and RWE Back German Nuclear Startup Proxima Fusion at €2.4 Billion Valuation
summary: German startup Proxima Fusion has raised €411 million ($469 million) from a range of investors, including national energy firm RWE AG and Alphabet Inc.’s Google, to develop a nuclear fusion plant it hopes will be operational in the 2030s.

title: Samsung Results Spur Stock Rotation to Less-Loved Sectors | Insight with Haslinda Amin 07/07/2026
summary: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Mubadala Opens Credit Business to Outsiders
summary: Abu Dhabi's Mubadala Investment Co. is opening up one of its largest investment businesses to outside investors for the first time, Dinesh Nair reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: PBOC Unveils New Measures to Cement HK's Yuan Hub Status | The China Show | 7/6/2026
summary: “Bloomberg: The China Show” is your definitive source for news and analysis on the world's second-biggest economy. From politics and policy to tech and trends, Yvonne Man and Avril Hong give global investors unique insight, delivering in-depth discussions with the newsmakers who matter.  (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Adnoc to Buy Shell's South African Fuel Stations
summary: Adnoc Distribution is has agreed to buy Shell fuel stations across South Africa in a deal valued at $1 billion. CEO Bader Saeed Al Lamki explained the rationale behind the move to Bloomberg's Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East and Africa. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: German Industry Grows More Than Expected on Auto Sector
summary: German industrial production grew for a second month, another sign that Europe’s largest economy is exiting the drag from the Iran war.

title: Oil Climbs as Fresh Tanker Strike Highlights Risks Around Hormuz
summary: Oil gained following attacks on shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting continued risks to vessels in the critical waterway.

title: Temasek-Backed Gym Chain Cult.Fit Files Paperwork for India IPO
summary: Cult.Fit Ltd. has filed draft paperwork to raise about $100 million in an initial public offering, a rare debut for a gym operator in the world’s most populous country.

title: FTSE 100 Outperforms as Shell Drives Gains
Modi Meets Prabowo to Strengthen Ties
summary:
Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi is in Jakarta, where he and Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto are set to sign up to eight agreements spanning defense, health, education and technology.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Can Australia Become Asia-Pacific's AI Hub?
summary: Australia is emerging as a leading destination for AI data centers, but the window to secure that advantage may be closing. Deloitte Access Economics says the country faces a "sliding doors moment" to become Asia-Pacific's AI infrastructure hub. John O'Mahony discusses what's at stake on 'Bloomberg: The Asia Trade.' (Source: Bloomberg)

title: PBOC Unveils Measures to Cement Hong Kong’s Yuan Hub Status
summary: China’s central bank announced a slew of measures aimed at enhancing Hong Kong’s role in promoting overseas use of the yuan as well as boosting the city’s financial connectivity with the mainland.

title: Won’s 24-Hour Trading Debut Passes With Below-Average Volume
summary: Trading in the Korean won passed with below-average volume in its first day of round-the-clock dealing during a largely uneventful session, compared to the past 30 days.

title: Samsung’s Record Profit Fails to Impress After AI Chip Rally
summary: Samsung Electronics Co.’s quarterly profit surged 19-fold but failed to impress investors used to eye-catching growth numbers from the suppliers of chips to the global AI boom.

title: Early Forint Bull Barclays Sees Rally Fading as Euro Work Begins
summary: The forint rally triggered by Hungary’s political shift is fading as Prime Minister Peter Magyar faces a daunting task to prepare the country for euro accession, according to a strategist at Barclays Plc.

title: Buy SK Hynix’s New ADRs and Sell Seoul-Listed Shares, UBS Says
summary: Investors should purchase SK Hynix Inc.’s planned American depositary receipts and sell the chipmaker’s South Korean-traded stock as the new securities are likely to trade at a premium, according to UBS Group AG.

title: China Buys More US Soybeans as Key Agricultural Trade Ramps Up
summary: China has purchased more soybeans from the US, according to people familiar with the matter, as the key agricultural trade gains momentum following a summit between the leaders of the two nations in May.

title: Hong Kong Begins Trial Operation of New Gold Clearing System
summary: Hong Kong has launched the trial operation of a new gold clearing system, backed by several major banks, in a key step toward its ambition to become a significant bullion-trading hub with price-setting power.

title: US Talks Tested as LNG Ship Struck in Hormuz Strait
summary: A laden liquefied natural gas carrier was hit by a projectile near the Omani coast as it exited the Strait of Hormuz, heightening unease among shipowners and testing a US-Iran agreement intended to halt attacks in the waterway. Bloomberg's Stuart Livingstone-Wallace breaks down the situation. (Source: Bloomberg)
